title: OSC 1337 — iTerm2 proprietary control sequences
summary: OSC 1337 is iTerm2's umbrella for proprietary features, taking a key=value (or key=value:base64payload) argument: OSC 1337 ; key=value ST. Notable sub-keys include SetUserVar, CurrentDir, RemoteHost, File (inline images, base64 in the payload after a ':'), and ShellIntegrationVersion. It is a single-vendor extension though several terminals have adopted parts of it.
source_version: iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes documentation, retrieved 2026-05-29

attribution.note: iTerm2 documents OSC 1337 as its proprietary escape-code namespace: OSC 1337 ; key=value ST. Keys include SetUserVar, CurrentDir, RemoteHost, File (inline images), ShellIntegrationVersion, and others.

frame.note: OSC frame (ECMA-48). 1337 is iTerm2's proprietary command number; no registry. Arguments are 'key=value' (some keys carry a ':base64' payload).

terminator_detail.note: ECMA-48 mandates ST; xterm and most modern terminals also accept BEL. Emit ST, accept both.

params.name: File — inline images / file transfer
params.meaning: OSC 1337 ; File=key=value;... : <base64 data> ST renders an image inline (or transfers a file). Args include inline=1, width, height, name (base64), size.
params.byte_sequence_ST_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; F i l e = i n l i n e = 1 : <base64> ESC \   ==  \x1b]1337;File=inline=1:<b64>\x1b\\   (inline image)
params.byte_sequence_BEL_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; F i l e = i n l i n e = 1 : <base64> BEL

params.name: SetUserVar — user variable
params.meaning: OSC 1337 ; SetUserVar=name=<base64 value> ST sets a user-defined variable that iTerm2 status bars / badges can reference.
params.byte_sequence_ST_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; S e t U s e r V a r = g i t = <base64> ESC \   ==  \x1b]1337;SetUserVar=git=<b64>\x1b\\
params.byte_sequence_BEL_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; S e t U s e r V a r = g i t = <base64> BEL

params.name: CurrentDir — working directory
params.meaning: OSC 1337 ; CurrentDir=/path ST tells iTerm2 the shell's current directory (used for new-tab-in-same-dir, etc.). OSC 7 is the cross-terminal equivalent.
params.byte_sequence_ST_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; C u r r e n t D i r = / h o m e / m e ESC \   ==  \x1b]1337;CurrentDir=/home/me\x1b\\
params.byte_sequence_BEL_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; C u r r e n t D i r = / h o m e / m e BEL

params.name: RemoteHost — user@host
params.meaning: OSC 1337 ; RemoteHost=user@host ST reports the logged-in remote host so iTerm2 can show it / detect ssh sessions.
params.byte_sequence_ST_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; R e m o t e H o s t = m e @ s e r v e r ESC \   ==  \x1b]1337;RemoteHost=me@server\x1b\\
params.byte_sequence_BEL_readable: ESC ] 1 3 3 7 ; R e m o t e H o s t = m e @ s e r v e r BEL

gotchas: OSC 1337 is PROPRIETARY to iTerm2; do not assume other terminals implement it. WezTerm and a few others support the File (image) and SetUserVar subsets, but coverage is partial.
The File payload is base64-encoded image bytes after the ':'; values like name= are themselves base64. Get the inner encoding right or the image fails silently.
For portable inline images prefer the kitty graphics protocol or sixel where supported; for working-directory reporting prefer OSC 7 over CurrentDir.
Some keys (e.g. CurrentDir, RemoteHost) overlap with cross-terminal mechanisms (OSC 7, OSC 133); emit the portable one when you can.
Over ssh/tmux the sequence must be passed through (tmux requires the passthrough escape or it strips it).

v1_smoke_test.asserts: OSC 1337 File/SetUserVar/CurrentDir/RemoteHost render as byte-exact OSC strings in ST and BEL forms; proprietary single-vendor status recorded.
